JL
MEMBERS ENROLLED TO REPRESENT AXPAYERS
BEFORE TEE INTERNAL REVENUE SERMCE
Apil 6, 1992
Mr. Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca 95804-0807
RE: Verbal advice from John Wallace, Counsel, regarding use of funds of the
California Society of Enrolled Agents Political Action Committee for costs
of a contract lobbyist. Additionally, some specific questions regarding
allowable use of PAC funds.
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
On April l, 1992, l-ee Borton, EA, an involved member of our Society, contacted Mr.
Wallace on our behalf. He asked Mr. Wallace whether funds of a Political Action Committee
(PAC) could be used to pay the costs of a contract lobbyist. Mr. Wallace indicated that such
use of PAC funds was appropriate, and provided Mr. Borton the "general guidance" letter
oft'ered to the California Association of Management, dated September 04, 1990. Additionally,
he suggested that a formal letter be drafted describing our specific situation. This letter
represents our request to FPPC for a written opinion.
Fact Situation #1: The California Society of Enrolled Agents, a tax-exempt business
associahon under Internal Revenue Code Section 501(c)(6), currently has a separate segregated
Political Action Committee (California registration # 860167) funded by voluntary contributions
from our Members. The Society also retains the services of a contract lobbyist (Valentine,
Brown Associates), whose job it is to monitor legislation which affects our members, advocate
on our behalf with elected and governmental officials and advise us on campaign contributions
which might assist in furthering the politicai interests of the Society's membership.
Questign fl: May the costs of the contract lobbyist be paid from the funds of the PAC?
Fact Situation #2: The Irgislative Committee of the California Society of Enrolled
Agenrs holds periodic prrlirical training sessions in different regions of the State of California
The purpose of these sessions is to train interested Society members to become more effective
in dealing with legislaiive action whictr innpacts the Society's political interests. The political
AFFILIATE
@


training sessions are free to interested members. Costs incurred for these sessions generally
include the transportation and lodging of instructors and a luncheon for the attendees.
Ouestion #2: Can the costs of these political training sessions be paid from the funds of
the PAC?
Fact Situation #3: Periodically, newsletters (Capitol Beat, vmple copies enclosed) are
provided to individual members who are contributors to the PAC. These newsletters do not
include advertising and contain information which politically aware members ofour Society frnd
interesting. They contain both informational data and occasional calls for political action (eg.,
writing a letter to an elected official).
Ouestion #3: May the costs of developing, printing and sending the political newsletter
be paid from the funds of the PAC?
If you need clarification on any of the situations or questions in this letter, please contact
Lee Borton, EA at 7141592-2061. Please send your reply to me at the above address.
Thank you for your attention to these questions. The answers will assist us in staying
within acceptable parameters of political action.
Sincerely,
2Z&z
Mark D. Olander, EA
Officer, CSEA-PAC
cc: ke Borton, EA
Jim Stem, EA - Chairman, CSEA lrgislative Committee; Chairman, CSEA-PAC
Jack Robishaw, EA - President, CSEA
Ted Bernardi, EA - Member, CSEA-PAC
J. Howard Moore, EA - Member, CSEA-PAC
William G. Sprague, CMP - Executive Vice President, CSEA

sections.question:
Question #1: May the costs of the contract lobbyist be paid from the funds of the PAC?

Question #2: Can the costs of these political training sessions be paid from the funds of the PAC?

Question #3: May the costs of developing, printing and sending the political newsletter be paid from the funds of the PAC?
sections.question_synthetic: The California Society of Enrolled Agents seeks FPPC guidance on whether PAC funds can be used to pay for: (1) contract lobbyist services, (2) political training sessions for members, and (3) development and distribution of political newsletters.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an organization to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains three fact situations followed by three corresponding questions. No FPPC conclusion or response is included in this document.

Question #2: Can the costs of these political training sessions be paid from the funds of the PAC?

Question #3: May the costs of developing, printing and sending the political newsletter be paid from the funds of the PAC?

embedding.summary: The California Society of Enrolled Agents submits a request to the FPPC for written guidance on three specific questions regarding permissible uses of their Political Action Committee funds, including lobbyist costs, political training sessions, and political newsletters.
